problem.instruction: Read the passages in <A> and <B>, and follow the directions.
problem.passage:
<A>
Project-based learning (PBL) is a teaching method that facilitates students to use an inquiry process with an integrated goal and interrelated subsidiary tasks. One possible procedure for implementing PBL is provided below.

Students collaboratively set the goal and scope of the project. This makes students feel in control of their own projects from the beginning. Once the goal is set, students as a group actively discuss and decide upon what to include in their project. When collecting information for the project, students develop integrated language skills in meaningful ways. Students then create their projects collaboratively with their group members. Finally, students present their projects in class. When assessing student projects, the teacher evaluates students' learning progress, focusing on the process as well as the product.

<B>
Referring to the procedure as described in <A>, Ms. Park, a middle school English teacher, implemented PBL into her class over six weeks. Each week, one class session was allocated for the PBL project. When each session was over, Ms. Park briefly wrote a teacher's log to record events and observations. Some entries of her logs are provided below.

Week 1: I decided on a specific goal for the project and announced it to students. The goal was to make tourist brochures and distribute them to the local communities. I assigned students to groups of four. I also provided guidelines on the project.

Week 2: The groups explored possible destinations to include in their brochures. Students also searched the Internet for various brochures and analyzed the sections within. They found details including attractions, activities, and food.

Week 3: The groups conducted a survey on their classmates' recommendations for the destination their group decided upon. They did so by asking and responding to each other. Then they summarized the survey results.

Week 4: The students worked closely in a group to make their brochures. Upon completion, they prepared for a group presentation.

Week 5: Each group gave a ten-minute presentation. Students also prepared for distributing the brochures to the local communities.

Week 6: As the final step, I evaluated students' brochures based on a rubric, which consisted of vocabulary, grammar, and layout.

Identify the TWO weeks in <B> that do NOT follow the procedure provided in <A>. Then, explain how the identified weeks deviate from the procedure in <A>.

steps.label: 범위 설정 — PBL 절차 5단계 확인
steps.explanation:
<A>에 제시된 PBL 절차 핵심 원칙:
(1) 학생들이 collaboratively 목표와 범위를 설정
(2) 그룹이 포함 내용을 논의·결정
(3) 정보 수집 → 통합적 언어 기술 발달
(4) 그룹이 collaboratively 결과물 제작
(5) 수업에서 발표; 교사는 process와 product 모두에 초점을 맞춰 평가
<B>의 6주 실행에서 위 원칙을 위반한 주차 두 개를 찾는 것이 과제.

steps.label: 이론↔지문 Week별 대조 — Week 2~5 확인
steps.explanation:
Week 2: 정보 탐색·분석 → (3) 정보 수집 단계에 부합. ✓
Week 3: 설문 실시·요약 → 추가 정보 수집 및 언어 기술 사용. ✓
Week 4: 그룹 제작 + 발표 준비 → (4) 협력 제작 단계. ✓
Week 5: 발표 + 지역 배포 준비 → (5) 발표 단계. ✓
Week 1과 Week 6이 문제 주차 후보로 남는다.

steps.label: Week 1 이탈 분석 — 목표 설정 주체
steps.explanation: <A>: 학생들이 collaboratively 목표와 범위를 설정해야 한다('Students collaboratively set the goal and scope'). <B> Week 1: Ms. Park이 직접 목표를 결정('I decided on a specific goal')하고 학생들에게 발표만 했다. 학생들은 목표 설정 과정에 전혀 참여하지 못했으므로 collaboratively set the goal 원칙에 위반된다.

steps.label: Week 6 이탈 분석 — 평가 범위
steps.explanation: <A>: 교사는 학습 진도를 평가하되 process와 product 모두에 초점을 맞춘다('focusing on the process as well as the product'). <B> Week 6: Ms. Park은 루브릭(어휘, 문법, 레이아웃)을 기반으로 최종 결과물(brochures)만 평가했다. 탐구·협업·발표 등 process 평가가 누락되었으므로 PBL 평가 원칙에 위반된다.

steps.label: 전략 메모 — 함정 구별
steps.explanation:
함정 주의:
① Week 1 — 목표(tourist brochures)가 교사 주도로 설정. '그룹 배정'이나 '가이드라인 제공'은 부수적 문제가 아니라 목표 설정 주체가 핵심.
② Week 6 — 루브릭 자체가 문제가 아니라 루브릭이 최종 product만 다룬다는 점이 핵심. process 평가(예: 협업 과정, 탐구 과정) 부재가 이탈 근거.
③ Week 5 배포 준비 — <A>에 '지역사회 배포'가 명시되지 않으나, <A>의 발표 단계와 어긋나지 않으므로 이탈 아님.

key_terms.definition_en: In PBL assessment, the learning progress, collaboration, and inquiry activities students engage in throughout the project, as opposed to only the final product.
key_terms.definition_ko: PBL 평가에서 최종 결과물과 달리, 학생들이 프로젝트 수행 과정에서 보이는 학습 진도·협업·탐구 활동.
key_terms.exam_usage: Week 6: Ms. Park이 brochure(product)만 평가하고 process 평가를 누락

key_terms.definition_en: The final tangible outcome of a PBL project — the artifact students create and present.
key_terms.definition_ko: PBL 프로젝트의 최종 유형 결과물. 학생들이 만들어 발표하는 완성 작품.
key_terms.exam_usage: Week 6에서 루브릭으로 평가한 brochure가 product에 해당

key_terms.definition_en: Working jointly with others in a shared endeavor; in PBL, students collaboratively set the goal and create the project together.
key_terms.definition_ko: PBL에서 학생들이 함께 목표를 설정하고 결과물을 만들어 가는 협력적 작업 방식.
key_terms.exam_usage: Week 1: Ms. Park이 단독으로 목표를 설정 — collaboratively set the goal 원칙 위반

model_answer.essay_content: Week 1 and Week 6 do not follow the PBL procedure. In Week 1, Ms. Park predetermined the project goal and announced it to students, whereas in <A>, students should collaboratively set the goal and scope of the project. In Week 6, Ms. Park evaluated only the final product based on a rubric, whereas in <A>, the teacher should assess both the learning process and the product.

scoring_criteria.criterion: Week 1 이탈 설명: 교사 단독 목표 설정 vs. 학생 collaboratively 설정
Week 6 이탈 설명: 결과물(product)만 평가 vs. process + product 평가

variants.label: 답안 A
variants.content: Week 1 and Week 6 do not align with the PBL procedure. In Week 1, Ms. Park directly sets and announces the project goal to the students, instead of allowing them to collaboratively determine the goal and scope of the project. In Week 6, she evaluates students' learning progress based solely on the final products — brochures — without assessing both the learning process and the product.

variants.label: 답안 B
variants.content: Week 1: In <A>, students should collaboratively set the project goal, but in <B>, Ms. Park predetermined the goal and announced it to the students. Week 6: In <A>, the teacher should evaluate both the process and product, but in <B>, Ms. Park evaluated only the final product based on a rubric.

references.author: Thomas, J. W.
references.title: A Review of Research on Project-Based Learning
references.excerpt: In project-based learning, students are the primary agents of their projects. The process of inquiry and collaboratively setting goals at the outset is essential to PBL's success; when teachers predetermine the goals and hand them to students, the motivational and cognitive benefits of PBL are undermined. Moreover, effective PBL assessment must attend to both the process — including collaboration, inquiry, and revision — and the final product.

references.author: Stoller, F. L.
references.title: Establishing a Theoretical Foundation for Project-Based Learning in Second and Foreign Language Contexts
references.publisher: In G. H. Buck & K. Riordan (Eds.), Project-Based Learning in Second Language Acquisition (pp. 11–50). Information Age Publishing
references.excerpt: PBL evaluation should be ongoing and multidimensional, capturing student growth in the process — collaboration, language use during tasks — as well as the quality of the product. Limiting assessment to the final artifact misses much of what makes PBL educationally powerful. Students' active participation in goal-setting reflects core principles of learner agency that distinguish PBL from conventional instruction.

source.concept_explanation:
Project-Based Learning(PBL)의 두 핵심 원칙:

① 학생 주도 목표 설정: 학생들이 collaboratively 프로젝트 목표와 범위를 설정한다. 교사가 일방적으로 목표를 지정하면 PBL의 핵심인 학생 주체성(student agency)이 무너진다.

② 과정 + 결과물 통합 평가: PBL에서 교사는 최종 product뿐 아니라 탐구·협업·발표 등 학습 process 전반을 평가한다. product만 평가하면 PBL이 아니라 일반 과제 제출과 다르지 않다.

본 문제 핵심 이탈:
- Week 1: Ms. Park이 단독으로 목표를 설정 → collaboratively set the goal 원칙 위반
- Week 6: 루브릭으로 brochure(product)만 평가 → process 평가 누락
